title: Erythrocephalum marginatum
intro: Erythrocephalum marginatum is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as a wildflower or herb. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties, which can help in the treatment of fever, wounds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Erythrocephalum marginatum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is typically prepared as a decoction or infused in hot water to make a tea, or sometimes used as a topical ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.
